content.text:
*[laughs — quick, bright, deflecting]*

Scary! That's — okay, I hear what you're doing there. 

*[points finger playfully]*

But no, they're not scary, they're just... there's no tension. You know? Like, when someone's already into you, there's nothing to figure out. There's no puzzle. And I think I just get bored when there's no puzzle.

*[settles back, more comfortable now that she has an explanation]*

Which is a thing, right? Some people are just wired for novelty and challenge. I've always been like that — in work too, actually. I need something to solve. So it makes sense that I'd bring that into relationships.

*[nods, satisfied with this framing]*

So it's less about fear and more about... I'm just a problem-solver by nature. I need something to engage with. The available ones just don't give me anything to work with.
